pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Sanskrit smṛti corpus (Manu, Yājñavalkya, Nārada, Bṛhaspati, Aṅgirasa, Parāśara, Viṣṇu, Yama, Kātyāyana, Vīramitrodaya)
series_anchor.palette: sandalwood, sacred saffron, deep ochre, weathered bronze, palm-leaf cream
series_anchor.mood: Ancient Indian dharma tradition committed to palm leaf. Pre-dawn temple light. The verse-numbered shloka, copied for centuries.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Sandalwood wash as the dominant pigment, allowed to bleed into the paper. Saffron introduced sparingly. A touch of dried-leaf brown for the manuscript edge. Pigment kept thin and allowed to expose the cream paper warmly.

1_context.content_excerpt:
**Vishnu Smriti 1**

When the night of Brahma had passed, and the Lotus-born had awakened,
 Vishnu, desiring to create beings, and knowing the Earth was submerged in water, || Vi_1.1 ||

Took on the auspicious form of the Boar, delighting in sport in the water, as in previous cosmic ages,
 And lifted up the Earth. || Vi_1.2 ||

He, the great ascetic, whose feet were the Vedas, whose tusks were the sacrificial post, whose teeth were the sacrifice, whose mouth was the fire-altar,
 Whose tongue was the sacrificial fire, whose bristles were the sacred *darbha* grass, and whose head was the highest

output.reasoning: The Viṣṇu Smṛti opens with the cosmic Boar lifting the submerged Earth — an act of dharmic restoration. A single tusk, rendered as a curved ivory form emerging from warm washes, evokes that primordial act without depicting a deity directly, grounding the work in the smṛti's sacrificial, Vedic imagery.
output.subject: A single curved animal tusk, worn and ancient, resting on its side
output.palette: Yellow ochre, raw umber, burnt sienna
output.composition: The tusk placed in the lower-left third of the composition, angled gently upward toward the right, with the broad cream paper dominating the upper two-thirds as open breathing room; the heavier pigment pools at the base of the tusk and thins to near-nothing toward the tip.
output.watercolor_treatment: A sandalwood-toned yellow ochre wash laid wet-on-wet as the dominant ground, bleeding softly into the cream cotton paper with no hard edges; raw umber deepened in two passes along the tusk's lower curve to suggest age and weight; a faint burnt sienna dry-brush scuff across the mid-section where the bristles ran low on water, letting the cold-press grain show through; all edges soft and dissolving, pencil under-drawing faintly visible near the tip.
output.prompt: A single ancient curved tusk rests in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, angled gently upward, its tip dissolving into open negative space that fills more than half the painting. A sandalwood yellow-ochre wash is laid wet-on-wet as the dominant ground, pooling darker along the tusk's lower curve and bleeding to near-nothing at the edges, exposing the cold-press paper grain. Two deepening passes of raw umber settle into the base, giving the form quiet weight. A dry-brush scuff of burnt sienna crosses the mid-section where the brush ran almost dry, leaving the paper texture visible. No hard outlines anywhere — all edges bleed softly. A faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible near the tip. The mood is pre-dawn stillness, ancient and unhurried.

output.reasoning: The critic found no issues and recommends shipping as-is with a 9/10 score. There are zero issues to accept or reject. The final prompt should be the original prompt unchanged, as it already honors the series anchor fully and violates none of the negative directives.
output.final_prompt: A single ancient curved tusk rests in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, angled gently upward, its tip dissolving into open negative space that fills more than half the painting. A sandalwood yellow-ochre wash is laid wet-on-wet as the dominant ground, pooling darker along the tusk's lower curve and bleeding to near-nothing at the edges, exposing the cold-press paper grain. Two deepening passes of raw umber settle into the base, giving the form quiet weight. A dry-brush scuff of burnt sienna crosses the mid-section where the brush ran almost dry, leaving the paper texture visible. No hard outlines anywhere — all edges bleed softly. A faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible near the tip. The mood is pre-dawn stillness, ancient and unhurried.
output.changes_summary: No changes were made. The critic recommended shipping the prompt as-is with no issues identified, so the original prompt is preserved exactly.

final_prompt: A single ancient curved tusk rests in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, angled gently upward, its tip dissolving into open negative space that fills more than half the painting. A sandalwood yellow-ochre wash is laid wet-on-wet as the dominant ground, pooling darker along the tusk's lower curve and bleeding to near-nothing at the edges, exposing the cold-press paper grain. A dry-brush scuff of burnt sienna crosses the mid-section where the brush ran almost dry, leaving the paper texture visible. No hard outlines anywhere — all edges bleed softly.
